claim: Small models (350M-1B) are memory-bound, task-specific, and latency-sensitive; embedding layers in distilled models are 60%+ of parameters, inefficient for reasoning
actionable: When designing small models for on-device deployment, minimize embedding layer size through vocabulary reduction; prioritize reasoning parameters over embedding parameters
source_title: Everything I Learned Training Frontier Small Models — Maxime Labonne, Liquid AI

claim: Gated short convolutions 2x-3x faster than sliding window attention or gated linear attention on-device; better latency than transformer alternatives
actionable: For on-device small models, use gated short convolution blocks instead of sliding window attention to achieve 2-3x inference speedup
source_title: Everything I Learned Training Frontier Small Models — Maxime Labonne, Liquid AI

claim: LFM 2.5 350M pre-trained on 28T tokens exceeds Chinchilla scaling laws; test time scaling laws suggest even more pre-training optimal
actionable: When training small models, allocate generous pre-training data beyond Chinchilla laws; scaling laws show benefits at 350M parameters with 28T tokens
source_title: Everything I Learned Training Frontier Small Models — Maxime Labonne, Liquid AI

claim: Doom loops (infinite repetition) in small reasoning models reduced 15%->~0% via DPO then RL; Qwen 3.5 0.8B has 50%+ doom loops
actionable: When training small reasoning models, implement diversity in temperature sampling and n-gram repetition penalties in RL to combat doom loops
source_title: Everything I Learned Training Frontier Small Models — Maxime Labonne, Liquid AI

claim: DPO preference alignment during on-policy data generation with temperature sampling and LLM jury scoring effectively mitigates doom loops
actionable: When using DPO, generate diverse rollouts with temperature sampling and use LLM scoring to pick best/worst pairs, avoiding doom loop examples
source_title: Everything I Learned Training Frontier Small Models — Maxime Labonne, Liquid AI

claim: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards + n-gram repetition penalty nearly eliminates doom loops in reasoning models
actionable: For small reasoning models, combine RL with explicit reward for answer existence and lightweight repetition penalties to prevent infinite loops
source_title: Everything I Learned Training Frontier Small Models — Maxime Labonne, Liquid AI

claim: Small models excel at tool use and agentic tasks when paired with web search; knowledge gaps bridged by tools rather than memorization
actionable: For on-device agents, deploy small models with tool access for search/APIs rather than large models relying on memorization
source_title: Everything I Learned Training Frontier Small Models — Maxime Labonne, Liquid AI

claim: SFT cold start data essential for RL success; missing SFT examples for a task predicts RL training failure; restart with augmented SFT
actionable: When RL fails on a task, check if SFT training included similar examples; add SFT data before restarting RL training
source_title: Everything I Learned Training Frontier Small Models — Maxime Labonne, Liquid AI

claim: On-device deployment benefits: no internet needed (in-car), low latency, privacy for regulated sectors (finance, healthcare)
actionable: When deploying models in safety-critical or privacy-sensitive domains, prioritize on-device small models over cloud APIs
source_title: Everything I Learned Training Frontier Small Models — Maxime Labonne, Liquid AI
